title: The Ordinary Routine for Beginners: What to Actually Buy
description: The Ordinary is cheap and effective, but the huge catalog is overwhelming and some products clash. Here's a simple, no-conflict starter routine — which to buy first and how to layer them.
painQuote: I'm new to skincare and The Ordinary has a hundred products — which do I actually need?
intro: The Ordinary makes genuinely good products at unbeatable prices — but the catalog is huge, the names are chemistry labels, and some actives shouldn't be layered together. No wonder beginners freeze.
You don't need ten bottles. Here's a simple starter routine that covers the basics, plus the few combinations to avoid.

sections.h2: Don't buy ten things at once
body: The fastest way to irritate your skin (and waste money) is grabbing five actives because a video said to. Start with a small, non-conflicting set and add slowly.
Pick by your actual goal — hydration, oil/pores, or fading marks — not by what's trending.
Hyaluronic Acid 2% + B5 for lightweight hydration (AM and PM, on damp skin). Niacinamide 10% + Zinc for oil and pores in the morning. Azelaic Acid 10% at night for texture, redness, and fading marks.
That trio covers most beginners without clashing.
Apply water-based, thinnest products first, then thicker ones, then seal with moisturizer. So: hyaluronic acid → niacinamide (AM) or azelaic (PM) → moisturizer.
Always finish the morning with sunscreen — The Ordinary doesn't make one, so that's your one other-brand essential.

Don't pile multiple strong actives in the same routine when you're starting — e.g., avoid layering several acids, or vitamin C with niacinamide at the exact same time if it irritates you. Alternate them across AM/PM or different days.
Want a foolproof, no-guesswork routine? Take the quiz and we'll sequence it for you.

faq.q: Which The Ordinary product should I buy first?
faq.a: If you're unsure, start with Hyaluronic Acid 2% + B5 (hydration, suits everyone) and Niacinamide 10% + Zinc (oil and pores). Add a targeted active like Azelaic Acid once your skin is comfortable.

faq.q: Can I use The Ordinary products together?
faq.a: Many yes, but not all at full strength in one routine. Layer thin-to-thick, introduce one at a time, and split strong actives between morning and night to avoid irritation.

faq.q: Do I still need other brands?
faq.a: Yes — at minimum a sunscreen, since The Ordinary doesn't make one. A good lightweight SPF every morning is non-negotiable.
